title: SEO in Perth
meta_title: SEO Perth | Get Found in Local Search | OptiTide
meta_description: SEO for Perth businesses, done properly. Local search, Google Business Profile and on-page work from $750 a month including GST, with no lock-in contract.
intro: If someone in Perth searches for what you sell and finds a competitor instead, that is a solvable problem. Here is what SEO actually involves, how long it really takes, and what it costs.
body_html:
What SEO is actually doing

SEO is the work of making a website the answer Google reaches for when someone searches. There is nothing mystical about it. Google has to be able to find your pages, read them, and decide they are a better answer than the alternatives. Most sites that do not rank are failing at one of those three things, and which one it is changes the work entirely.

That is worth saying plainly, because SEO is a field with a lot of noise in it. Nobody controls Google's ranking algorithm. Anyone who guarantees you a position is either targeting phrases nobody searches for, or doing something that will eventually cost you.

Why local search is a different game

A Perth business competing for "web design" is up against the entire English-speaking internet. The same business competing for "web design Perth" is up against the businesses down the road. That is a fight worth having, and it is the one most local businesses should be picking.

Local search also behaves differently. For a lot of service searches Google shows a map with three businesses above the ordinary results. Those three positions often take more of the clicks than everything beneath them, and getting into that block depends heavily on your Google Business Profile rather than your website alone.

  • A claimed and completed Google Business Profile, with the right categories and service areas.
  • Your business name, address and phone written identically everywhere they appear online.
  • Pages on your site that actually mention the suburbs and services you want to be found for, rather than leaving Google to guess.
  • Reviews, which you have to earn — there is no shortcut here that is worth the risk.

Where the work usually starts

Before writing anything new, it is worth finding out whether Google can read what is already there. A site can look perfect to a visitor and be effectively invisible to a search engine. If you want to check that yourself first, the walkthrough on why a website is not showing on Google covers how to test it.

After that, the work generally falls into three areas. Technical: can Google crawl and index the site, does it load quickly, does it work properly on a phone. On-page: does each page target something a real person types, and does it answer that thing properly. Off-page: does anything else on the internet link to you, which is still one of the strongest signals Google has.

How long it takes

Longer than anyone wants. Technical fixes can show up within weeks because you are removing a blockage. Content and authority take months, because Google is watching how people respond to your pages over time and that data does not exist on day one.

A reasonable expectation is that you will see movement in impressions before you see movement in enquiries, and that meaningful change in competitive terms takes several months of consistent work. Anyone promising page one in thirty days is selling something else.

This is also why SEO suits a business that intends to still be trading in two years. If you need enquiries this week, SEO is not the lever — that is what advertising is for.

What it costs

SEO runs at $750, $1,500 or $2,500 a month. All prices are in AUD, include GST, and there is no lock-in contract, so you can stop if it is not working for you.

Which tier suits you depends mostly on how competitive your market is and how much ground there is to make up. A sole trader in a specific suburb needs far less than a business competing across the metro area against companies with their own marketing staff. Ask and you will get a straight answer, including when the answer is that the cheapest tier is enough for now.

If the site itself is the problem — slow, hard to use on a phone, or built in a way search engines struggle with — web design and development starts at $750 one-off, and fixing the foundation first is usually cheaper than paying monthly to work around it.

Working with someone on your time zone

OptiTide is based in Perth, Western Australia and works to AWST, Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM. A question sent at 3 PM lands in the middle of the working day rather than at the end of someone else's. The late finish is deliberate: something you send after closing can still be picked up that evening.

The work is done remotely and there is no office to visit. You get a client portal instead, where you can view and pay invoices, follow what is happening and message us without digging through an email thread.

Where to start

Tell us the searches you want to win and what you are seeing now. You will get an honest read on whether SEO is the right spend, which tier fits, and what would need to change first. Compare the options on the services page, read the detail on SEO, or get in touch.

faqs.q: How much does SEO cost in Perth?
faqs.a: OptiTide's SEO runs at $750, $1,500 or $2,500 a month, in AUD and including GST, with no lock-in contract. Which tier fits depends on how competitive your market is and how much needs fixing. You can stop at any time, so the arrangement has to keep earning its place.

faqs.q: How long before I see results from SEO?
faqs.a: Technical fixes can show up within weeks because they remove a blockage. Content and authority take months, because Google needs time to see how people respond to your pages. Expect impressions to move before enquiries do. Anyone promising page one in thirty days is not describing SEO.

faqs.q: Can you guarantee I'll rank number one?
faqs.a: No, and neither can anyone else — nobody controls Google's algorithm. A guarantee usually means targeting phrases nobody searches for, or tactics that risk a penalty later. What you get here is month-to-month work with no lock-in, so you are free to stop if it is not paying off.

faqs.q: Do I need SEO if I already have a Google Business Profile?
faqs.a: They do different jobs. Your Business Profile is what puts you in the map block for local searches, and it is free to claim, so it is worth doing regardless. SEO is about the website itself ranking in the ordinary results, which is where people land when they want to read more before contacting you.

faqs.q: Is my website good enough for SEO, or does it need rebuilding first?
faqs.a: Sometimes the honest answer is that the site is the bottleneck. If it is slow, awkward on a phone, or built so search engines struggle to read it, paying monthly to work around that is worse value than fixing it. A website build starts at $750 one-off, and you will get a straight answer about which order makes sense.

faqs.q: Are you actually based in Perth?
faqs.a: Yes. OptiTide is based in Perth, Western Australia and works to AWST, Monday to Friday, 9 AM to 9 PM. The work is done remotely so there is no office to drop into, but you are dealing with someone in your own time zone rather than a call centre in another one.
